hnote_items_addressed: Item 2 — Verdict distribution bar audit (no-op finding)
hnote_items_deferred: Item 3 — Lightweight 4-row falsification module (separate substantive block; recommended for next analyst run as EXP-256)
Item 4 — How-to-Evaluate 5-question preface (separate prose block; recommended for run after Item 3 lands so hero composition is settled)
rework_summary: EXP-261 is the curmudgeon-corrected reissue of EXP-255. Substance of design (thesis-led hero, eclipse badge, deferred Items 3/4) preserved unchanged. Four corrections applied: (1) FC1 + FC2 find anchors now use ds- prefix matching the live codebase (ds-scorecard / ds-sc-hero / ds-sc-card / ds-sc-number / ds-sc-label / ds-sc-sublabel; .ds-sc-domains, .ds-verdict-bars). (2) FC1 verification gate fixed — post-condition grep now targets ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero (the actual class string) and is paired with a positive grep for ds-thesis-hero >= 1, so a no-op patch can no longer pass verification. (3) All new CSS classes renamed with ds- prefix (ds-thesis-hero / ds-thesis-statement / ds-thesis-meta / ds-thesis-badge / ds-thesis-figures / ds-tf-item / ds-tf-number / ds-tf-label / ds-tf-link / ds-tf-accent / ds-tf-textnum) + keyframe ds-thesis-badge-pulse, restoring the project's design-system invariant. (4) Pulse-ring color now reads from a CSS custom property (--ds-thesis-pulse-rgb) toggled in the prefers-color-scheme:dark @media block, eliminating the dark-mode regression flagged in curmudgeon hole #5. The 'zero falsifications' phrase loss is acknowledged honestly in the corrected load_bearing_content_audit (the rhetorical work is carried forward by 'Survive scrutiny: 0' in the figures row).
target: build-scripts/generate-html.js — Overview tab hero composition. Replaces the four-up ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero block (lines ~1441–1461) with a thesis-led hero (display-scale operator-authored sentence + eclipse 'Live test pending' badge + body-type stat row of the same four headline figures). Adds new CSS rules for the .ds-thesis-hero treatment to the CSS const between .ds-sc-domains and .ds-verdict-bars. Verdict bar chart, accuracy-math-section, and downstream Overview content are unchanged.
source: Curmudgeon bounce-back of EXP-255 + HNOTE-OPERATOR-DESIGN-REVIEW-V2-OVERVIEW-REWORK-001 + external round-2 design review (monitor/analyst/design-review-2026-04-25-external.html)
curmudgeon_review: pending — spot-check requested per curmudgeon recommendation (NOT full re-review)

audit_findings_item_2.question: Reviewer reports 'six adjacent stat tiles' as canonical distribution view despite EXP-209 (Phase A1) shipping the verdict bar chart.
audit_findings_item_2.investigation: Inherited from EXP-255 audit, re-confirmed against the current generate-html.js (post-ds-prefix unification): line 1439 invokes ${generateVerdictBarChart(...)}; line 1441 begins the four-up ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero block. No six-tile verdict-distribution display exists.
findings_verbatim: Line 1439 (current): ${generateVerdictBarChart(tally, wins.length)} renders the canonical 6-row verdict distribution bar chart. It IS on Overview, ABOVE the four-up ds-sc-hero block.
Line 1441 (current): <div class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero" style="grid-template-columns:repeat(4,1fr)"> begins the FOUR-tile scorecard. ds- prefix confirmed.
No six-tile verdict-distribution display exists anywhere on Overview. The only six-element verdict block is the bar chart itself.
audit_findings_item_2.audit_verdict: Item 2 is a NO-OP for redundancy removal. The reviewer's 'six adjacent stat tiles' was a misread of the four-up scorecard. Curmudgeon c1 independently verified this audit and accepted it.

hero_swap_design.layout_principle: Thesis at display scale → supporting figures at body scale. Visual hierarchy says 'this is the claim, here are the numbers that back it' rather than the dashboard-feel of four equal-weight tiles.
hero_swap_design.thesis_sentence_verbatim: None of the ${counts.total} predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain.
hero_swap_design.thesis_sentence_provenance: Operator-authored in HNOTE-OPERATOR-DESIGN-REVIEW-V2-OVERVIEW-REWORK-001. Wording preserved verbatim except the literal '69' parameterized to ${counts.total} per project rule against hardcoding derived numbers (CLAUDE.md). Today's live count = 71. The HNOTE 'no derived content' clause is interpreted as 'do not editorialize the meaning' — substituting the live count is data hygiene, not content drift. Curmudgeon c1 accepted this reading; no objection on the rework.
hero_swap_design.thesis_treatment: clamp(1.85rem, 3.6vw, 2.65rem) serif heading-color, weight 600, letter-spacing -0.012em, line-height 1.18, max-width 32ch with text-wrap:balance. Above the fold on 1440x900 desktop alongside the badge and stats row.
hero_swap_design.badge_treatment: Pill chip, accent border-color, accent text-color, transparent background, with a small pulsing accent-color dot for liveness. Hover inverts to filled accent. prefers-reduced-motion disables the pulse. Pulse ring color now reads from --ds-thesis-pulse-rgb (toggled in dark mode) — fixes curmudgeon hole #5 dark-mode regression. Links to #p4-426 (section 4.2.6 'What would change our verdict' — the eclipse pre-commitment subsection) and switches to the 'pages' tab.
hero_swap_design.badge_link_target_rationale: Section 4.2.6 (id="p4-426") is the eclipse pre-commitment subsection. Curmudgeon c1 independently verified both p4-426 and p4-427 are live in docs/index.html (EXP-254 already integrated). Link is stable today and tomorrow.
hero_swap_design.figures_treatment: Four-column grid below the thesis, separated by a 1px rule-color top border. Each item: serif 1.55rem number + sans-serif 0.78rem uppercase letterspaced label + (where applicable) a small Details link. Mobile breakpoints at 760px (2 cols) and 420px (1 col).
hero_swap_design.accessibility: ds-thesis-statement gets id="ds-thesis-statement" used by section aria-labelledby. Badge gets descriptive aria-label naming the date and target section. Figures list uses role="list" with aria-label="Headline figures". Animation respects prefers-reduced-motion. Color contrast for accent text on transparent and white-on-accent on hover both audit clean against WCAG AA in the existing palette.

changes_from_exp255.before: class="scorecard sc-hero" + sc-card / sc-number / sc-label / sc-sublabel (no ds- prefix)
changes_from_exp255.after: class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero" + ds-sc-card / ds-sc-number / ds-sc-label / ds-sc-sublabel
changes_from_exp255.rationale: Codebase uses ds- prefix throughout. Re-grepped against live build-scripts/generate-html.js to confirm exact strings: grep -c 'class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero"' returns 1 (line 1441); grep -c 'class="scorecard sc-hero"' returns 0. EXP-255 anchors would have failed exact_string_replace.
Re-grepped against live CSS const: lines 550–551 have ds- prefix. EXP-255 anchor would have failed.

changes_from_exp255.before: grep -c 'class="scorecard sc-hero"' build-scripts/generate-html.js  # expect 0 — TAUTOLOGICAL (already 0 before patch)
changes_from_exp255.after: grep -c 'class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero"' build-scripts/generate-html.js  # expect 0 (only after patch); paired with grep -c 'class="ds-thesis-hero"' build-scripts/generate-html.js  # expect >= 1
changes_from_exp255.rationale: Curmudgeon hole #2: the EXP-255 negative gate would pass even if FC1 failed, because the actual class string is ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero, not scorecard sc-hero. Fixed by targeting the real string. Positive gate also strengthened to require ds-thesis-hero present.

changes_from_exp255.before: thesis-hero / thesis-statement / thesis-meta / thesis-badge / thesis-badge-dot / thesis-badge-pre / thesis-badge-date / thesis-figures / tf-item / tf-number / tf-textnum / tf-accent / tf-label / tf-link; @keyframes thesis-badge-pulse
changes_from_exp255.after: ds-thesis-hero / ds-thesis-statement / ds-thesis-meta / ds-thesis-badge / ds-thesis-badge-dot / ds-thesis-badge-pre / ds-thesis-badge-date / ds-thesis-figures / ds-tf-item / ds-tf-number / ds-tf-textnum / ds-tf-accent / ds-tf-label / ds-tf-link; @keyframes ds-thesis-badge-pulse
changes_from_exp255.rationale: Curmudgeon hole #3: design-system invariant requires ds- prefix on all project classes. EXP-255 introduced 11 unprefixed classes + 1 unprefixed keyframe. All renamed.

changes_from_exp255.before: rgba(74,111,165,...) hardcoded in keyframe — light-mode --accent only
changes_from_exp255.after: rgba(var(--ds-thesis-pulse-rgb),...) where --ds-thesis-pulse-rgb is 74,111,165 by default and 143,175,212 inside @media(prefers-color-scheme:dark)
changes_from_exp255.rationale: Curmudgeon hole #5: pulse ring stayed light-blue in dark mode while the dot itself correctly switched to dark-mode accent. CSS custom property toggle keeps the keyframe rule generic and matches the rest of the file's dark-mode discipline.

changes_from_exp255.before: Claimed 'zero falsifications' phrase preserved in accuracy-math-section directly below.
changes_from_exp255.after: Acknowledges 'zero falsifications' phrase is removed from the Overview tab. Rhetorical work carried by 'Survive scrutiny: 0' figure. Accuracy-math-section uses different phrasing ('only X acknowledged falsifications').
changes_from_exp255.rationale: Curmudgeon hole #4: the literal phrase exists exactly once in generate-html.js (line 1445 sublabel, deleted by FC1) and is not in accuracy-math-section. Audit corrected to acknowledge the small content loss.

changes_from_exp255.before: thesis ~85px tall × 2 lines = ~170px (curmudgeon hole #6 flagged this as wrong)
changes_from_exp255.after: thesis at clamp(1.85rem,3.6vw,2.65rem) → 42.4px computed on 1440x900; line-height 1.18 → ~50px per line; 104-char thesis at max-width 32ch wraps to ~3-4 lines on most screens → ~150-200px thesis. Plus 36px badge meta + ~90px figures row = ~280-330px hero. Title block above the hero adds ~120px → ~400-450px total to the bottom of the figures row. Comfortably above 900px desktop fold; ~360px on iPhone fold.
changes_from_exp255.rationale: Curmudgeon hole #6: re-derived from actual CSS values rather than guessed pixel heights. Conclusion (above the fold) unchanged; estimate is now defensible.

changes_from_exp255.before: Three figures use 1.55rem serif numbers; fourth (timestamp) uses 1.05rem text 'Wrong side' — flagged as asymmetric (curmudgeon hole #7, polish call)
changes_from_exp255.after: Preserved as 'Wrong side' for now — curmudgeon explicitly did NOT block on this. Polish-call alternatives noted in deferred_polish_options below.
changes_from_exp255.rationale: Curmudgeon recommended exploring 'Wrong side' → date or compact-link demotion, but flagged it as 'does not block'. Keeping ds-tf-textnum class for now (renamed from tf-textnum). If a follow-up EXP wants to retire it and unify all four figures under ds-tf-number, the class is structurally separable.

file_changes.verification_after: grep -c 'class="ds-thesis-hero"' build-scripts/generate-html.js  # expect >= 1 (positive gate); grep -c 'class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero"' build-scripts/generate-html.js  # expect 0 (negative gate, only true AFTER patch); grep -c 'id="ds-thesis-statement"' build-scripts/generate-html.js  # expect >= 1
file_changes.grep_preflight: Run BEFORE applying: grep -c 'class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero"' build-scripts/generate-html.js  # MUST return 1; if 0, find anchor is stale and patch will be a no-op — STOP and re-grep the actual current strings.

file_changes.find_anchor_description: Insert new .ds-thesis-hero CSS rules into the CSS const, immediately after the .ds-sc-domains rule (line 550) and before the .ds-verdict-bars rule (line 551). The find string spans both lines exactly as they appear today.
file_changes.verification_after: grep -c '\.ds-thesis-hero{' build-scripts/generate-html.js  # expect >= 1; grep -c '@keyframes ds-thesis-badge-pulse' build-scripts/generate-html.js  # expect >= 1; node build.js html  # build should pass; grep -c 'class="ds-thesis-hero"' docs/index.html  # expect 1 after build
file_changes.grep_preflight: Run BEFORE applying: grep -c '.ds-sc-domains{grid-template-columns:repeat(3,1fr)}' build-scripts/generate-html.js  # MUST return 1; if 0, find anchor is stale — STOP and re-grep.

word_count_summary.note: Visual hierarchy is up; word count is down. Body-row labels are tightened ('Claimed wins' vs 'Claimed "Wins"' + sublabel sentence). The expanded prose that accompanied each tile is intentionally removed because the thesis sentence above replaces it.

load_bearing_content_audit.purpose: Per HNOTE curmudgeon focus + curmudgeon hole #4 correction: verify the hero swap doesn't lose any load-bearing content from the original four-up stat tiles, AND honestly account for the 'zero falsifications' phrase that the original audit overclaimed as preserved.

items.original: The author claims ${counts.total} confirmed predictions and zero falsifications
items.in_new_hero: PARTIAL: 'Claimed wins: ${counts.total}' carries the headline number. The literal phrase 'zero falsifications' is REMOVED from the Overview tab — it appeared exactly once in generate-html.js (line 1445 sublabel, inside the FC1 deletion). EXP-255's audit incorrectly claimed it was preserved in accuracy-math-section; in fact accuracy-math-section uses different phrasing ('only X acknowledged falsifications'). The rhetorical work of the phrase is carried forward by the new figures row: 'Survive scrutiny: 0' explicitly contradicts a 'zero falsifications' claim with a 0 of its own, and 'Acknowledged + silent failures: ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}+${silentFailures}' is the numerical refutation. Adding the phrase back to accuracy-math-section would mildly violate this EXP's out_of_scope (accuracy-math-section is preserved untouched). Conclusion: small content loss accepted; the rhetorical purpose is achieved by the new figure pair.

items.original: Not one claim produces a result that the globe model can't explain and the dome uniquely can
items.in_new_hero: REPROMOTED. This sentence WAS the demoted version of the thesis. The new thesis sentence ('None of the ${counts.total} predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain') is the same idea at display scale. Nothing lost; the meaning is now hero-scale instead of body-tile-scale.

items.original: He admits ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}. We found ${silentFailures} more he silently removed.
items.in_new_hero: PRESERVED: numbers preserved as ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}+${silentFailures}. Detail link preserved (text 'Details →' to #p3-failures). The 'he admits / silently removed' framing is fully present in the accuracy-math-section directly below (untouched by this patch).

items.original: His blockchain proof timestamps the observations, not the predictions. As of April 2026, the author concedes this distinction in writing — but hasn't fixed the underlying proof structure.
items.in_new_hero: PRESERVED: headline 'Wrong side / Timestamp error' preserved as the fourth figure with Details link. The full prose is in the dedicated Timestamp Error tab (untouched) and in the latest-findings BREAKING NEWS block on Overview (untouched, lines below the new hero). No load-bearing content lost.

load_bearing_content_audit.conclusion: All load-bearing content remains accessible from the Overview within one scroll or one tab-click, EXCEPT the literal phrase 'zero falsifications' which is removed (its rhetorical purpose is carried by 'Survive scrutiny: 0' in the figures row). The hero compresses the supporting figures into a body row but no claim or number is removed.

kernel_of_truth.what_round_1_brief_got_right: The original four-up dashboard treatment was deliberately stat-heavy because the round-1 reviewer flagged a credibility gap that came from the document NOT putting numbers up front. The four tiles fixed that and pushed credibility from C+ to B+ (round-1 → round-2). It is fair to acknowledge that the dashboard-feel WAS load-bearing — without it the document had read as opinion-led.
kernel_of_truth.why_the_swap_still_works: Round-2's improvement now allows demoting the dashboard without losing the credibility gain. The numbers stay (in the body row); what changes is the visual weight given to opinion-vs-data. With the thesis sentence at hero scale, a reader sees the claim immediately and the supporting figures within the same viewport — the document still 'leads with numbers' in the sense that they're above the fold and within one glance, but the editorial claim is no longer subordinated to four equally-weighted tiles. The round-2 designer's framing — 'this single change does more for credibility than every body-text improvement combined' — is the operator's reading too, and the audit-of-content above shows nothing material is lost.

why_kernel_doesnt_break_swap: The round-1 dashboard-feel was a fix for a specific failure mode (numbers not up-front). That failure no longer applies because (a) the verdict bar chart above the new hero shows the actual distribution numerically, (b) the body-row figures preserve all four headline numbers above the fold, (c) the accuracy-math-section directly below provides the audit numbers in detail. The dashboard's job is now done by three other surfaces.

anticipated_objections.objection: The thesis sentence hardcodes a number ('69') that drifts as the dome adds wins; today's count is 71.
anticipated_objections.response: Implementation uses ${counts.total} (live template variable), not a hardcoded literal. Per CLAUDE.md 'never hardcode a number that can be derived from the data' AND per operator HNOTE language 'numbers stay; only their visual hierarchy changes', substituting the template variable preserves the sentence structure exactly while honoring data hygiene. Curmudgeon c1 accepted this reading; no objection on the rework.

anticipated_objections.objection: Dashboard-feel was deliberate (round-1 #F1 deferred to Phase C). Removing it is reverting a previous decision under round-2 designer pressure without independent justification.
anticipated_objections.response: Operator HNOTE explicitly elevates this from Phase C to active work and gives the rationale: 'the current 69 / 0 / 4+4 / Wrong Side four-up at hero scale presents stat tiles that don't add to a coherent thesis — the actual thesis is none of the 69 survives, and the thesis sentence belongs at display scale, not below the stats.' Round-2 designer's praise is independent confirmation. Curmudgeon c1 stress-tested this decision and did not block.

anticipated_objections.objection: The eclipse badge competes with the thesis for hero attention.
anticipated_objections.response: Designed for calmness: pill outline (no fill until hover), accent border on transparent background, sub-body-size text (0.82rem), small pulsing dot for liveness without movement-noise (and respects prefers-reduced-motion). Sits BELOW the thesis sentence in the meta row, not beside it.

anticipated_objections.objection: The pulsing dot animation is gratuitous and may trigger vestibular sensitivity.
anticipated_objections.response: @media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce) disables the keyframe explicitly. The animation amplitude is sub-pixel ring expansion at 2.4s period, not movement of the dot itself — the dot is stationary; only its box-shadow ring expands and fades. The pulse-ring color now also adapts to dark mode via --ds-thesis-pulse-rgb. If the curmudgeon objects, removing it is a one-line CSS deletion and degrades cleanly to a static dot.

anticipated_objections.objection: Linking the badge to #p4-426 specifically is fragile if section numbering changes.
anticipated_objections.response: Verified by curmudgeon c1: both p4-426 and p4-427 anchors exist in built docs/index.html (EXP-254 has integrated; 4.2.6 keeps its eclipse-pre-commitment role). Stable today and tomorrow.

anticipated_objections.objection: Items 3 and 4 are deferred; the operator wanted the full Overview rework.
anticipated_objections.response: HNOTE explicitly permits chunking. Curmudgeon c1 accepted the chunking as 'sound' (chunking_rationale_check). Recommended sequencing unchanged: this EXP through curmudgeon spot-check → integrate → next analyst run picks up Item 3 → run after that picks up Item 4.

anticipated_objections.objection: Does this rework preserve the curmudgeon-flagged design strengths from EXP-255?
anticipated_objections.response: Yes. The thesis-led composition, the eclipse badge, the figures row, the accessibility plumbing, and the chunking choice are unchanged in substance. Curmudgeon c1's kernel_of_truth ('the design IS sound rhetorically; accessibility plumbing is competently done; Item 2 audit correctly resolved as no-op; chunking minimizes integration risk; badge link target verifiably stable') applies unchanged to EXP-261. Only the execution-layer bugs (find anchors, verification gate, ds- prefix, dark-mode pulse) are corrected.

deferred_polish_options.option: Replace 'Wrong side' textnum with a date/word that fits ds-tf-number style — e.g., 'Apr 7' (the date the author conceded the timestamp distinction) or 'Apr 2026' (month). Allows retirement of ds-tf-textnum and unifies all four figures under one number style.
deferred_polish_options.curmudgeon_finding: hole #7 (minor, polish call, not a blocker)
deferred_polish_options.decision: Defer. ds-tf-textnum is structurally separable from the rest of the figures row and can be removed in a follow-up EXP without touching the find anchors of this rework.

deferred_polish_options.option: Replace timestamp-error figure cell with a compact link below the three numeric figures rather than a full figure cell.

holes_addressed: Round-2 review's primary concentrated gap (overview / first viewport) — addressed via thesis swap (Item 1).
Phantom-redundancy concern about verdict-distribution display — investigated and dismissed (Item 2 audit, re-confirmed).
Missing visible eclipse pre-commitment surface above the fold — addressed via badge (Item 5).
Round-1 deferred F1 (now elevated to active per HNOTE) — addressed.
Curmudgeon c1 hole #1 (broken FC1+FC2 find anchors) — fixed: find strings now use exact ds- prefixed class names, verified by grep against live source.
Curmudgeon c1 hole #2 (tautological FC1 verification gate) — fixed: post-condition grep targets ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero (real string) and is paired with positive grep for ds-thesis-hero.
Curmudgeon c1 hole #3 (ds- prefix violation on new classes) — fixed: all 11 new classes + keyframe renamed with ds- prefix.

Curmudgeon c1 hole #4 (load_bearing_content_audit overclaimed 'zero falsifications' preservation) — fixed: audit honestly acknowledges the phrase is removed; rhetorical purpose now explicitly carried by 'Survive scrutiny: 0' figure.
Curmudgeon c1 hole #5 (pulse keyframe hardcoded light-mode RGB) — fixed: pulse ring color now reads from --ds-thesis-pulse-rgb CSS variable, toggled in prefers-color-scheme:dark @media block.
Curmudgeon c1 hole #6 (above-the-fold height estimate guessed not derived) — fixed: estimate now derived from actual CSS values; conclusion (above fold) unchanged.
Curmudgeon c1 hole #7 (ds-tf-textnum visual asymmetry) — acknowledged as polish call, deferred to follow-up EXP per curmudgeon's own non-blocking framing.
new_evidence_added: Re-grep of build-scripts/generate-html.js post-ds-prefix unification confirms FC1 anchor exists at line 1441 and FC2 anchor exists at lines 550-551.
Curmudgeon c1 kernel_of_truth analysis incorporated as confirmation that design substance is sound; EXP-261 only fixes execution-layer bugs.
cross_references: EXP-255 — parent proposal, curmudgeon-rejected for execution-layer bugs (substance preserved here).
EXP-254 (section 4.2.7 eclipse geometry) — already integrated; badge link target #p4-426 confirmed stable.
EXP-209 (Phase A1 verdict bar chart) — Item 2 audit relies on this being already canonical on Overview.
monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/EXP-255-proposal.json — the curmudgeon c1 review whose findings drove this rework.
ISS-1571, ISS-1572, ISS-1573, ISS-1574 — issues this EXP resolves on integration.

decider_instructions.integration_step_0_preflight: Before applying patches, verify find anchors are still present in the current build-scripts/generate-html.js: grep -c 'class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero"' build-scripts/generate-html.js (must return 1) AND grep -c '.ds-sc-domains{grid-template-columns:repeat(3,1fr)}' build-scripts/generate-html.js (must return 1). If either returns 0, STOP — anchors have drifted since this proposal was written and need re-grepping.
decider_instructions.integration_step_1: Apply FC1 (HTML hero replacement) via exact_string_replace in build-scripts/generate-html.js. Verify post-condition: grep -c 'class="ds-thesis-hero"' build-scripts/generate-html.js (expect >= 1) AND grep -c 'class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero"' build-scripts/generate-html.js (expect 0). FAIL THE BUILD if either fails.
decider_instructions.integration_step_2: Apply FC2 (CSS rule insertion) via exact_string_replace. Verify with: grep -c '\.ds-thesis-hero{' build-scripts/generate-html.js (expect >= 1) AND grep -c '@keyframes ds-thesis-badge-pulse' build-scripts/generate-html.js (expect >= 1). FAIL THE BUILD if either fails.
decider_instructions.integration_step_3: Run build: node build.js html. Build must succeed without warnings.
decider_instructions.integration_step_4: Verify rendered HTML: grep -c 'ds-thesis-statement' docs/index.html (expect >= 1); grep -c 'ds-thesis-badge' docs/index.html (expect >= 1); grep -c 'ds-thesis-hero' docs/index.html (expect >= 1).
decider_instructions.integration_step_5: Run tests: node test.js. All existing assertions should still pass. (No test.js patches in this EXP — design surface only; the curmudgeon may request test additions.)
decider_instructions.fail_loud_directive: If exact_string_replace returns 'no match' on either FC1 or FC2, treat as HARD FAILURE. Do NOT continue with downstream verification — the page would be unchanged after build and acceptance_criteria would silently pass against unpatched DOM. This is the structural failure mode that bit EXP-255 (curmudgeon hole #2 + structural recommendation in deeper_analysis).
decider_instructions.routing: queue-for-curmudgeon-spot-check (NOT full re-review) per curmudgeon c1 recommendation. Then integrate.

curmudgeon_review_focus: Spot-check only: confirm the four execution-layer fixes (find anchors, verification gate, ds- prefix on new classes, dark-mode pulse via CSS variable) all landed correctly in this rework.
Confirm the load_bearing_content_audit no longer overclaims 'zero falsifications' preservation.
Optional: re-verify the above-the-fold derivation against actual CSS values.
Substance of design (thesis-led composition, badge to p4-426, deferred Items 3/4) was already approved in c1 — do not re-litigate.

build_impact.additive_lines_estimate: ~26 CSS rule lines (added 2 for --ds-thesis-pulse-rgb light/dark) + ~22 HTML template lines = ~48 lines added.
build_impact.file_size_change_estimate: <2 KB net increase in generate-html.js; <2 KB net increase in built docs/index.html.
build_impact.pdf_impact: PDF is built via Playwright headless Chromium loading the built HTML. The new .ds-thesis-hero CSS uses standard properties supported by Chromium PDF rendering. PDF will render the static state of the badge (dot static, no pulse), which is acceptable.

acceptance_criteria: build-scripts/generate-html.js no longer contains class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero".
build-scripts/generate-html.js CSS const contains the .ds-thesis-hero rule between .ds-sc-domains and .ds-verdict-bars (preserving the existing rule order).
build-scripts/generate-html.js contains @keyframes ds-thesis-badge-pulse exactly once and references rgba(var(--ds-thesis-pulse-rgb),...) (not hardcoded RGB).
Built docs/index.html contains the rendered thesis sentence with substituted ${counts.total} value (today: 71).
Built docs/index.html contains the badge with href="#p4-426" and onclick="showTab('pages');return false".
